atoms.semantic_claim: The downstream diff for crates/optimism/consensus/src/proof.rs introduces behavior or wiring that must be accounted for in the v2.2 merge target.

destinations.snippet:
Preserved the upstream Reth `v2.2.0` workspace member list. No old
`crates/optimism/*`, `alloy-op-evm`, or `op-revm` paths were reintroduced.
destinations.evidence: The v2 port explicitly scoped OP execution and old optimism crates out of the fresh upstream skeleton.

destinations.snippet:
reth-evm = { path = "crates/evm/evm", default-features = false }
reth-evm-ethereum = { path = "crates/ethereum/evm", default-features = false }
destinations.evidence: The v2 workspace has Ethereum/Summit execution crates but no `crates/optimism/*` member for OP receipt-root behavior.

atoms.human_question: Confirm that OP receipt-root compatibility remains outside the current Reth v2.2/Summit merge scope. If OP support becomes in scope, this atom requires a dedicated OP stack port rather than a local one-file fix.
atoms.notes: Reclassified from missing to intentionally absent because the current v2 port explicitly omits old `crates/optimism/*` workspace members.

summary: The old OP Regolith/Canyon receipt-root behavior is intentionally absent from the current v2 port because OP execution is out of scope.
notes: This is not fixed by reintroducing a helper into the Summit/Ethereum port; it remains a human scope confirmation for future OP work.
